response:
To find the **characteristic polynomial** of a matrix $ A $, we compute the determinant of $ A - \lambda I $, where $ I $ is the identity matrix of the same size as $ A $, and then form the polynomial $ \det(A - \lambda I) $.

---

### Step 1: Define the matrix

Given:

$$
A = \begin{bmatrix}
3 & -12 & 13 \\
-6 & 13 & 11 \\
-7 & -6 & -8
\end{bmatrix}
$$

We compute the characteristic polynomial:

$$
\det(A - \lambda I) = \det\left( \begin{bmatrix}
3 - \lambda & -12 & 13 \\
-6 & 13 - \lambda & 11 \\
-7 & -6 & -8 - \lambda
\end{bmatrix} \right)
$$

---

### Step 2: Compute the determinant

We compute the determinant of the 3x3 matrix:

$$
\begin{vmatrix}
3 - \lambda & -12 & 13 \\
-6 & 13 - \lambda & 11 \\
-7 & -6 & -8 - \lambda
\end{vmatrix}
$$

We use cofactor expansion or row operations to simplify.

Let’s expand along the first row:

$$
\det(A - \lambda I) = (3 - \lambda) \cdot \begin{vmatrix}
13 - \lambda & 11 \\
-6 & -8 - \lambda
\end{vmatrix}
- (-12) \cdot \begin{vmatrix}
-6 & 11 \\
-7 & -8 - \lambda
\end{vmatrix}
+ 13 \cdot \begin{vmatrix}
-6 & 13 - \lambda \\
-7 & -6
\end{vmatrix}
$$

---

### Step 3: Compute each 2x2 determinant

1. $ \begin{vmatrix}
13 - \lambda & 11 \\
-6 & -8 - \lambda
\end{vmatrix} = (13 - \lambda)(-8 - \lambda) - (11)(-6) $

$$
= -104 - 13\lambda + 8\lambda + \lambda^2 +
